Wheeler refers to the cousin/uncle who drove the getaway car on Aug 24, 1955 after the wolf-whistle incident. Canonical: the driver was Maurice Wright per most Till-case historiography. Whisper "Fann" likely a degradation of "Maurice" (Whisper has lost the syllables) or a different family name. Surface-name resolution requires multi-model review.

pending_context.whisper: "Mr. the man that was on the truck with him out of Ohio"
pending_context.candidate_correction: [The Black] man that was on the truck with [Milam] out of [Ohio? Drew? Sunflower County?]
pending_context.notes: Wheeler is referring to one of two canonical Bryant-Milam farmhand witnesses: **Henry Lee Loggins** (the Black farmhand on the truck the night of the abduction; canonical "Too Tight" Levi Collins was the other) — both were sequestered by the Tallahatchie sheriff during the 1955 trial and never called to testify. Henry Lee Loggins later moved to Ohio (Dayton area) per post-2004 FBI re-investigation testimony. Wheeler's reference to "out of Ohio" + "his son out of New York" matches Loggins's biography. Canonical: **Henry Lee Loggins** (or Levi "Too Tight" Collins); the son in New York may refer to Loggins's son who was contacted in the 2004-07 FBI reopening.

Raw: "Where did you go? I planted uncles like uncle. The one they took me to his house. Just that about two years ago, he's 105." Whisper rendered "I planted at" or "I went to my" uncles' house. Speaker references the canonical Duck Hill MS uncle (his father's brother) who at the time of the 2011 interview was 105 years old.

pending_context.whisper: "if the moon in the night shan"
pending_context.candidate_correction: even if the moon in the night [is shining]"
pending_context.notes: Pass 2 125.P2.6 flagged this but the resolution is speaker-disambiguated: speaker is saying that the Mississippi Delta nights were so dark that even moonlight couldn't penetrate. Whisper "shan" is a degradation of "shines" or possibly "ain't shining."

Interviewer Joe Mosnier corrects his own date — Saturday in question is **Aug 27, 1955** (the canonical "Saturday in Greenwood shopping" pre-Aug-28-abduction Saturday); not Aug 25, 1955 (which was Thursday). No Whisper failure but worth flagging as canonical Till-case chronology: Aug 21 Sun (Wheeler's arrival in MS); Aug 24 Wed (Bryant's Grocery wolf-whistle); Aug 27 Sat (Greenwood shopping + Four Fifths jook joint); Aug 28 Sun morning ~2:30 AM (abduction).

And he let me drive.") Whisper "Jai" is uncertain — could be the same uncle as "Uncle Fann" (Aug 24 getaway driver) or a separate cousin. Family-genealogy resolution requires Wright-family multi-source check.
